subtitle: Team Health, Agile Maturity & Data-Driven Delivery
hero_sub: 15+ years enabling multiple Agile teams to deliver business value through consistent application of Agile principles — coaching teams and individuals to strengthen agile maturity, using metrics to guide continuous improvement, and removing impediments directly rather than just logging them.

the_case.answer: 15+ years of Scrum Master and Agile Coach roles across Eptura, Crocs, Understood.org, A&E, DigitasLBI/SunTrust, and more.
the_case.detail: Comfortably exceeds both the required and preferred thresholds — Scrum Master leadership has been the consistent throughline since 2014.

the_case.need: Strong knowledge of agile principles, servant leadership, facilitation, conflict resolution
the_case.answer: Core competency demonstrated across every engagement — coaching at both team and individual level, not just facilitating ceremonies.
the_case.detail: Servant leadership isn't a talking point — at Eptura I coached teams and Product Owners individually on backlog readiness and self-organization, advancing agile maturity across a ~10-team PMO.

the_case.need: Experience using metrics to improve team performance
the_case.answer: At Eptura, built and maintained Jira dashboards tracking velocity, sprint health, and WIP across a ~10-team program, using that data to guide performance improvements — driving a 35%+ efficiency gain and 20% sprint carryover reduction.
the_case.detail: The dashboards weren't a reporting exercise — they were how the team caught performance issues before they became delivery misses. That's the exact distinction this posting draws between data-driven decision-making and ceremony facilitation.

the_case.answer: Direct, daily fluency with all three across every recent engagement.
the_case.detail: Jira and Azure DevOps administration-level fluency, plus Excel for executive-facing KPI reporting at Eptura.

the_case.need: Leadership experience and familiarity with scaling frameworks
the_case.answer: SAFe-aligned program experience at DigitasLBI/SunTrust (2014) and Eptura (2022–2023).
the_case.detail: Scaling framework experience spans a decade, from a SOX-compliant financial-services SAFe program to a ~10-team enterprise SaaS PMO.

the_case.answer: Underline Communications: sold and established Agile from zero, delivering an enterprise SaaS platform upgrade in 16 weeks with a newly assembled team.
the_case.detail: This is direct proof of supporting agile transformation, not just operating inside an already-mature Agile organization — I've built the practice from scratch, not just maintained it.

the_stack.headline: The agile maturity toolkit.
the_stack.subhead: Tools used daily to turn data into team coaching conversations.

nodes.label: Jira & Dashboards
nodes.desc: Administration-level Jira fluency — velocity, WIP, sprint health dashboards
nodes.pm_role: Built and maintained flow-metrics dashboards at Eptura across a ~10-team program, driving a 35%+ efficiency gain.

nodes.label: Azure DevOps (ADO)
nodes.desc: Cloud delivery coordination for enterprise SaaS and retail environments
nodes.pm_role: Coordinated release readiness on Azure/Kubernetes infrastructure at Eptura, Crocs, and Understood.org.

nodes.label: Excel & Executive Reporting
nodes.desc: KPI reporting and portfolio-level status translation for senior leadership
nodes.pm_role: Produced recurring executive dashboards in Excel and Smartsheet at Eptura.

nodes.label: Claude Code & AI Tools
nodes.desc: Claude AI/Claude Code, ChatGPT, Jira AI, Microsoft Copilot
nodes.pm_role: Daily practitioner since 2022. Embedded AI tools directly into ceremonies at Crocs to cut administrative overhead ~30%.

nodes.label: Confluence & Smartsheet
nodes.pm_role: Used across every engagement for RAID logs and stakeholder documentation.

when_things_break.scenario: A team's performance is quietly declining but no one has the data to see it
response: At Eptura, built Jira dashboards tracking velocity, sprint health, and WIP across a ~10-team program
Used that data to guide coaching conversations, not just report status
Caught performance dips before they became missed commitments
Result: 35%+ increase in delivery efficiency, 20% reduction in sprint carryover

when_things_break.scenario: An organization has never run Agile and needs it built from zero
response: At Underline Communications, sold and established Agile from scratch for a team that had never used it
Built the ceremony cadence, backlog discipline, and coaching relationships from nothing
Assembled a newly formed distributed team around the new process
Result: delivered an enterprise SaaS platform upgrade in 16 weeks, on time and under budget

when_things_break.scenario: A distributed, multi-country team needs stabilization after inheriting a program in crisis
response: At Understood.org, inherited a distressed, multi-year program spanning 12+ countries
Took over in its final crucial months, under real pressure
Stabilized ceremony cadence and reporting transparency immediately
Result: delivered the replatform through QA/UAT with zero critical regressions

how_i_run_a_room: Every ceremony generates data, and that data drives coaching conversations, not just retro discussions
Impediments get removed directly, or teams get coached to resolve them independently — both are the job
Individual coaching happens alongside team coaching; agile maturity is a person-level outcome, not just a team-level one
RAID log maintained and reviewed at every stakeholder touchpoint; risks named before they become issues
Definition of Ready/Done enforced consistently, not treated as optional under deadline pressure
Collaborates with other Scrum Masters to evolve shared best practices, not operate in a silo

points: Built and maintained Jira dashboards tracking velocity, sprint health, and WIP
Coached teams and Product Owners at both team and individual level
Drove 35%+ increase in delivery efficiency, 20% reduction in sprint carryover
Facilitated Scrum-of-Scrums across ~10 distributed teams spanning India and California
Sold and established Agile from zero for an organization with no prior practice
Built ceremony cadence and coaching relationships from scratch
Delivered an enterprise SaaS platform upgrade in 16 weeks
Assembled and onboarded a newly formed distributed team
Facilitated ceremonies across Engineering, QA, Change Management, and Business
Reinforced Definition of Done via embedded compliance controls
Built 'Release Radar,' a real-time release-readiness tracking framework
Reduced administrative overhead ~30% via Jira AI and Copilot
Stabilized a distressed, multi-year program spanning 12+ countries
Steered a Sitecore-to-Contentful replatform to on-time launch
Primary point of contact for the VP of Engineering
Delivered through QA/UAT with zero critical release regressions

before: Ceremonies happen but agile maturity stays flat across teams
Team performance issues go unnoticed until they become delivery misses
Impediments get logged but not actually removed
Coaching happens at the team level only, never reaching individuals
after: Agile maturity advances because coaching happens at both team and individual level
Data surfaces performance issues early, before they become misses
Impediments get removed directly, or teams are coached to resolve them independently
A culture of accountability and quality reinforced consistently, not just when convenient

the_ramp.deliverable: Review current team health data, ceremony cadence, and backlog status for all supported teams; meet with Product Owners and leadership to understand delivery priorities.
Begin coaching at both team and individual level; identify the first team-health metric worth tracking more closely.
Own ceremony facilitation and reporting cadence for all supported teams; deliver the first data-driven process improvement recommendation.
